title: CLAUDE.md – hoopR Development Guide
text: hoopR R package providing clean, tidy men’s basketball play--play box score data. wraps NBA Stats API, ESPN API, KenPom, exporting 270+ functions across three primary data source families: nba_*(), espn_*(), kp_*(). Version: 3.0.0 (dev) R Requirement: >= 4.1.0 License: MIT Branch: devel active development, main releases

title: CLAUDE.md – hoopR Development Guide
code: # Regenerate roxygen documentation + NAMESPACE devtools::document()  # Run all tests devtools::test()  # Run a specific test file testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-nba_playbyplayv3.R")  # Full R CMD check devtools::check()  # Install locally devtools::install()  # Build pkgdown site locally pkgdown::build_site()

title: CLAUDE.md – hoopR Development Guide
code: R/                    # All package source code   nba_stats_pbp.R     # PBP functions: nba_pbp(), nba_pbps(), nba_playbyplayv3(), .players_on_court()   nba_stats_boxscore_v3.R  # All V3 boxscore wrappers + nba_boxscoresummaryv3()   nba_stats_leaders.R # Leader endpoints: nba_leagueleaders(), nba_dunkscoreleaders(), nba_gravityleaders()   nba_stats_league.R  # League endpoints: nba_leaguestandingsv3(), nba_iststandings(), etc.   nba_stats_roster.R  # Roster endpoints: nba_commonallplayers(), nba_commonteamyears(), etc.   nba_stats_scoreboard.R # Schedule/scoreboard: nba_schedule(), nba_scheduleleaguev2int(), etc.   nba_stats_team.R    # Team endpoints: nba_teamandplayersvsplayers(), etc.   nba_stats_video.R   # Video endpoints: nba_videodetailsasset(), nba_videoeventsasset(), etc.   utils_nba_stats.R   # nba_endpoint(), request_with_proxy(), HTTP helpers   utils.R             # make_hoopR_data(), pad_id(), general utilities   espn_*.R            # ESPN API wrappers   kp_*.R              # KenPom scrapers   nbagl_*.R           # NBA G-League API wrappers tests/testthat/       # Test files (150+)   helper-skip.R       # skip_nba_stats_test(), skip_espn_test(), skip_ncaa_mbb_test(), etc. man/                  # Auto-generated roxygen docs (DO NOT EDIT) data/                 # Package datasets NAMESPACE             # Auto-generated by roxygen2 (DO NOT EDIT)

title: CLAUDE.md – hoopR Development Guide
text: Every NBA Stats API wrapper follows pattern:
code: #' **Get NBA Stats API [Endpoint Name]** #' @name nba_functionname NULL #' @title #' **Get NBA Stats API [Endpoint Name]** #' @rdname nba_functionname #' @author Author Name #' @param game_id Game ID - 10-digit zero-padded ID (e.g., '0022200021') #' @param ... Additional arguments passed to an underlying function like httr. #' @return Returns a named list of data frames: Component1, Component2 #' #'    **Component1** #' #' #'    |col_name |types     | #'    |:--------|:---------| #'    |column1  |character | #'    |column2  |integer   | #' #' @importFrom jsonlite fromJSON toJSON #' @importFrom dplyr filter select rename bind_cols bind_rows as_tibble #' @import rvest #' @export #' @family NBA Boxscore V3 Functions #' @details #' ```r #'  nba_functionname(game_id = "0022200021") #' ``` nba_functionname <- function(game_id = "0022200021", ...) {   endpoint <- nba_endpoint('endpointname')   full_url <- endpoint   params <- list(GameID = pad_id(game_id))    df_list <- list()  # Always initialize before tryCatch    tryCatch(     expr = {       resp <- request_with_proxy(url = full_url, params = params, ...)       # Parse response...       # Apply janitor::clean_names()       # Apply make_hoopR_data()       # Assign to df_list     },     error = function(e) {       message(glue::glue("{Sys.time()}: Invalid arguments or no data for {pad_id(game_id)} available!"))     },     warning = function(w) {},     finally = {}   )   return(df_list) }

title: CLAUDE.md – hoopR Development Guide
text: make_hoopR_data() sets class c("hoopR_data", "tbl_df", "tbl", "data.table", "data.frame") attaches hoopR_timestamp hoopR_type attributes.
code: raw_data %>%   data.frame(stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%   dplyr::as_tibble() %>%   janitor::clean_names() %>%   make_hoopR_data("Description from NBA.com", Sys.time())

title: CLAUDE.md – hoopR Development Guide
text: V2: Returns resultSets[].headers[] + rowSet[][] (tabular). Parsed via nba_stats_map_result_sets(). V3: Returns nested JSON (e.g., game.actions[], boxScoreSummary.homeTeam.periods[]). Parsed via purrr::pluck() -> data.frame() -> pipeline. V3-style leader/standings endpoints: Return flat nested JSON arrays (e.g., dunks[], leaders[], teams[]). Parsed via purrr::pluck("key") -> dplyr::as_tibble() -> dplyr::mutate(across(everything(), .character)) -> janitor::clean_names() -> make_hoopR_data(). Examples: nba_dunkscoreleaders(), nba_gravityleaders(), nba_iststandings(). V3 PBP single personId per action (player1_id/player2_id/player3_id like V2). V3 substitutions: actionType="Substitution", personId=incoming, description contains “OutgoingPlayer”. V3 clock format: ISO 8601 duration "PT10M30.00S" ("MM:SS").

title: CLAUDE.md – hoopR Development Guide
text: Use %||% (rlang) null-safe defaults throughout:

title: CLAUDE.md – hoopR Development Guide
text: request_with_proxy() utils_nba_stats.R uses rvest::session() required NBA headers: - x-nba-stats-origin: stats - x-nba-stats-token: true - Referer: https://www.nba.com/ nba_endpoint() builds URLs via glue::glue('https://stats.nba.com/stats/{endpoint}') – validate internal endpoint list.

title: CLAUDE.md – hoopR Development Guide
text: Use expect_in() (subset check) rather expect_equal() (exact match) column validation. prevents test failures APIs add new columns: endpoints dynamic columns (like IST Standings game columns):
code: test_that("NBA Endpoint Name", {   skip_on_cran()   skip_on_ci()   skip_nba_stats_test()  # Requires NBA_STATS_TESTS=1    x <- nba_function(game_id = "0022200021")    cols_x1 <- c("col1", "col2", ...)   expect_in(sort(cols_x1), sort(colnames(x$Component)))   expect_s3_class(x$Component, "data.frame")    Sys.sleep(3)  # Rate limiting between tests }) expect_true(all(core_cols %in% colnames(x)))

title: CLAUDE.md – hoopR Development Guide
text: NBA Stats API: ~590 requests per 10 minutes. Always add Sys.sleep(3) end NBA Stats API test.

title: CLAUDE.md – hoopR Development Guide
text: Auto-generated roxygen2. Never edit manually. Run devtools::document() regenerate.

title: CLAUDE.md – hoopR Development Guide
text: Use Conventional Commits: Important: Never include AI agents assistants (e.g., Claude, Copilot) co-authors commits. Omit Co-Authored-trailers referencing AI tools.
code: feat: add nba_playbyplayv3() endpoint wrapper fix: correct V3 clock parsing for overtime periods docs: update NEWS.md for v3.0.0 test: add boxscoresummaryv3 column validation tests refactor: extract V3 substitution parsing into helper chore: update .Rbuildignore patterns ci: update GitHub Actions workflow versions

title: CLAUDE.md – hoopR Development Guide
text: pad_id() must called game IDs passing API (zero-pads 10 digits). V3 endpoints return different structures per endpoint – always verify nba_api Python parsers companion repo. data.frame(list_of_items) can produce unexpected column types API returns mixed NULL/value fields – use %||% defaults. @family tag groups functions pkgdown reference pages – keep consistent existing families. V3-style leader endpoints (dunkscoreleaders, gravityleaders) return values mixed types – coerce everything .character() %||% NA_character_ building data frames. IST Standings dynamic game columns (gameId1, gameId2, etc.) – tests use expect_true((core_cols %% colnames())) instead exact match. ESPN API columns change time – use expect_in() subset validation tests. NBA Stats V2 endpoints may return empty data certain games – tests handle empty results gracefully. Always initialize df_list <- list() tryCatch blocks avoid “object found” errors.

what: Adding a New NBA Stats API Endpoint
title: Contributing to hoopR
text: Use nba_endpoint() + request_with_proxy() HTTP Parse response purrr::pluck() -> data.frame() -> janitor::clean_names() -> make_hoopR_data() Use %||% (rlang) null safety every extracted field Add roxygen docs @export, @family, @return (including column markdown tables), @details example Create test tests/testthat/ skip_on_cran(), skip_on_ci(), skip_nba_stats_test() guards Update NEWS.md description new function Run devtools::document() update NAMESPACE

title: Contributing to hoopR
text: snake_case function names, variables, parameters Internal helpers (exported) prefixed . (e.g., .players_on_court_v3()) Parameter names use snake_case R, mapped PascalCase NBA Stats API (e.g., game_id -> GameID) Game IDs must always passed pad_id() API calls (zero-pads 10 digits) File names follow pattern R/nba_stats_*.R, R/espn_*.R, R/kp_*.R

title: Contributing to hoopR
text: V2 endpoints (e.g., boxscoretraditionalv2): Return resultSets[].headers[] + rowSet[][]. Parsed via nba_stats_map_result_sets(). V3 endpoints (e.g., boxscoretraditionalv3): Return nested JSON. Parsed via purrr::pluck() -> data.frame() -> pipeline. V3-style leader/standings endpoints (e.g., dunkscoreleaders, gravityleaders, iststandings): Return flat nested JSON arrays. Parse via purrr::pluck("key") -> dplyr::as_tibble() -> dplyr::mutate(across(everything(), .character)).

title: Contributing to hoopR
text: returned data frames must pass :
code: raw_data %>%   data.frame(stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%   dplyr::as_tibble() %>%   janitor::clean_names() %>%   make_hoopR_data("Description from NBA.com", Sys.time())

title: Contributing to hoopR
text: Every exported function needs: @name @rdname tags NULL object preceding function @title bold markdown description @author tag @param every parameter (including ...) @return markdown tables documenting column names types @importFrom specific function imports @export @family grouping pkgdown (e.g., "NBA PBP Functions", "NBA Boxscore V3 Functions") @details runnable example code block

title: Contributing to hoopR
text: Follow tidyverse style: snake_case, 2-space indentation Internal helpers start . (e.g., .players_on_court_v3()) Game IDs must passed pad_id() API calls Use %||% (rlang) null-safe defaults parsing API responses

title: Contributing to hoopR
text: Tests different API sources gated behind environment variables. Set running tests: Note: API tests skipped CRAN (skip_on_cran()) CI (skip_on_ci()) default. require network access valid API responses.
code: # Enable specific test suites Sys.setenv(NBA_STATS_TESTS = "1")     # NBA Stats API tests Sys.setenv(ESPN_TESTS = "1")          # ESPN API tests Sys.setenv(NBAGL_STATS_TESTS = "1")   # NBA G-League Stats tests Sys.setenv(NCAA_MBB_TESTS = "1")      # NCAA MBB tests Sys.setenv(KP_USER = "your_email")    # KenPom credentials Sys.setenv(KP_PW = "your_password")   # KenPom credentials  # Run all tests devtools::test()  # Run a single test file testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-nba_playbyplayv3.R")

title: Contributing to hoopR
text: writing tests NBA Stats API endpoints, follow pattern:
code: test_that("NBA Endpoint Name", {   skip_on_cran()   skip_on_ci()   skip_nba_stats_test()    x <- nba_function(param = "value")    cols_x1 <- c("col1", "col2", ...)   expect_equal(sort(colnames(x$Component)), sort(cols_x1))   expect_s3_class(x$Component, "data.frame")    Sys.sleep(3)  # Rate limiting - NBA API limit is ~590 req/10 min })

title: Contributing to hoopR
text: NBA Stats API rate limit approximately 590 requests per 10 minutes. Always add Sys.sleep(3) end NBA Stats API test avoid hitting rate limits test runs.

title: Getting Started with hoopR
text: generally speaking six men’s basketball data sources accessed package: hoopR-data repo NBA Stats Data API NBA G-League Stats Data API ESPN KenPom (Requires subscription access) NCAA

what: Function names indicate the data source
title: Getting Started with hoopR
text: Functions use hoopR-data repository contain load_ update_ function name considered loading functions play--play data, team box scores, player box scores. Functions use NBA Stats API start nba_ convention assumed get functions. hoopR version 3.0.0, package exports 127 functions covering NBA Stats API Functions use NBA G-League Stats API start nbagl_ convention assumed get functions. hoopR version 3.0.0, package exports 4 functions covering NBA G-League Stats API Functions use one ESPN’s APIs start espn_ convention assumed get functions. functions allow live access game data men’s college basketball NBA. hoopR version 3.0.0, package exports 10 functions covering men’s college basketball endpoints 10 NBA. Functions use Ken Pomeroy’s college basketball website start kp_ convention assumed get functions. functions allow users active subscription get access data tidy format. hoopR version 3.0.0, package exports 36 functions covering KenPom.com. Functions use NCAA website start ncaa_ convention assumed get functions. hoopR version 3.0.0, package exports 1 function(s) covering NCAA website, namely function access current NET rankings.

title: KenPom Example Functions
code: #---- kp_ratings_tables.R ---------------- pomeroy_ratings <- kp_pomeroy_ratings(min_year = 2020, max_year = 2020)  efficiency <- kp_efficiency(min_year = 2020, max_year = 2020)  fourfactors <- kp_fourfactors(min_year = 2020, max_year = 2020)  pointdist <- kp_pointdist(min_year = 2020, max_year = 2020)  height <- kp_height(min_year = 2020, max_year = 2020)  teamstats <- kp_teamstats(min_year = 2020, max_year = 2020)  playerstats <- kp_playerstats(metric = 'eFG', conf_only = FALSE, year = 2020)  kpoy <- kp_kpoy(year = 2020)

title: KenPom Example Functions
code: #---- kp_team_tables.R ------------------- team_sched <- kp_team_schedule(team = 'Florida St.', year = 2020)  gameplan <- kp_gameplan(team = 'Florida St.', year = 2020)  opptracker <- kp_opptracker(team = 'Florida St.', year = 2020)  team_players <- kp_team_players(team = 'Florida St.', year = 2020)  minutes_matrix <- kp_minutes_matrix(team = 'Florida St.', year = 2020)  team_player_stats <- kp_team_player_stats(team = 'Florida St.', year = 2020)  team_player_stats1 <- team_player_stats[[1]]  team_player_stats2 <- team_player_stats[[2]]  team_depth_chart <- kp_team_depth_chart(team = 'Florida St.', year = 2020)  team_lineups <- kp_team_lineups(team = 'Florida St.', year = 2020)

title: KenPom Example Functions
code: #---- kp_historical_tables.R ------------ team_history <- kp_team_history(team = 'Florida St.')  coach_history <- kp_coach_history(coach = 'Leonard Hamilton')  program_ratings <- kp_program_ratings()  # records_team appears to be working but there isn't anything # there anymore at the moment for all years # records_team <- get_records_team(browser, team = 'Florida St.')  pomeroy_archive_ratings <- kp_pomeroy_archive_ratings(date = '2018-11-22')  conf <- kp_conf(year = '2020',conf = 'ACC')  conf_stats <- kp_confstats(year = '2020')  conf_history <- kp_confhistory(conf = 'ACC')

title: KenPom Example Functions
code: #---- kp_misc_stats.R ------------------ trends <- kp_trends()   officials <- kp_officials(year = 2020)  hca <- kp_hca()   arenas <- kp_arenas(year = 2020)    game_attrs <- kp_game_attrs(year = 2020, attr = "Excitement")  fanmatch <- kp_fanmatch(date = "2020-03-10")

title: hoopR Copilot Instructions
text: hoopR R package (v3.0.0 dev) wraps NBA Stats API, ESPN API, KenPom. exports 270+ functions uses roxygen2 documentation, testthat testing, pkgdown documentation site.

title: hoopR Copilot Instructions
text: Follow tidyverse style: snake_case variables/functions, 2-space indentation. NBA Stats API functions use request_with_proxy() HTTP calls required NBA headers. Use %||% (rlang) null-safe defaults parsing API responses. returned data frames must pass janitor::clean_names() make_hoopR_data(). Use pad_id() game IDs passing API. Internal/non-exported helpers prefixed . (e.g., .players_on_court_v3()).

title: hoopR Copilot Instructions
text: Every exported function needs: @name @rdname tags NULL object @title bold markdown description @author tag @param every parameter (including ...) descriptive text (e.g., @param game_id Game ID - 10-digit zero-padded ID (e.g., '0022200021')) @return markdown tables documenting column names types data frame returned @importFrom specific function imports @export @family grouping pkgdown (e.g., “NBA PBP Functions”, “NBA Boxscore V3 Functions”) @details runnable example code block

title: hoopR Copilot Instructions
text: Use skip_on_cran(), skip_on_ci(), skip_nba_stats_test() guards. Validate columns expect_in(sort(expected_cols), sort(colnames(x))) (subset check, exact match). dynamic columns, use expect_true((core_cols %% colnames(x))). Add Sys.sleep(3) end NBA Stats API tests rate limiting (~590 req/10 min). Test game ID: "0022200021" "0022201086" known completed games. Handle empty API responses gracefully tests (V2 endpoints may return empty old games).

title: hoopR Copilot Instructions
text: Use format: type: description Types: feat, fix, docs, test, refactor, chore, style, perf, ci Important: Never include AI agents assistants (e.g., Claude, Copilot) co-authors commits. Omit Co-Authored-trailers referencing AI tools.

title: hoopR Copilot Instructions
text: V3 endpoints return nested JSON – use purrr::pluck() extraction. V3 PBP clock format "PT10M30.00S" "MM:SS". V3 PBP substitutions single personId (incoming) outgoing parsed description. V3 boxscore endpoints namespace: boxscoretraditionalv3, boxscoreadvancedv3, etc. V3-style leader/standings endpoints (dunkscoreleaders, gravityleaders, iststandings) return flat nested JSON arrays. Parse via purrr::pluck("key") -> dplyr::as_tibble() -> dplyr::mutate(across(everything(), .character)) -> janitor::clean_names(). V3-style schedule endpoint (scheduleleaguev2int) follows nested structure nba_schedule().

title: hoopR Copilot Instructions
text: Always initialize df_list <- list() tryCatch blocks. ESPN API columns change time – use subset validation tests. V3-style leader endpoints return mixed types – coerce .character() %||% NA_character_. IST Standings dynamic game columns – use expect_true((core_cols %% colnames())).

title: hoopR • Data and Tools for Men's Basketball
text: hoopR R package working men’s basketball data. package functions access live play play box score data ESPN shot locations available. version 1.3.0, hoopR also full NBA Stats API wrapper 127 functions added release. additionally scraping aggregating interface Ken Pomeroy’s men’s college basketball statistics website, kenpom.com. provides users active subscription capability scrape website tables analyze data .

title: Men's College Basketball win probability chart data from ESPN — espn_mbb_wp
text: espn_mbb_wp() - data frame 9 variables: game_id: character. Referencing game ID (game_id functions). play_id: character. Referencing play ID. period: integer. Clock (time) left game. time_left: character. Clock (time) left game. period_seconds_left: integer. Seconds left period. game_seconds_left: integer. Seconds left game. home_win_percentage: double. probability home team winning game. away_win_percentage: double. probability away team winning game (calculated 1 - home_win_percentage - tie_percentage). tie_percentage: double. probability game ending final period tie.

what: hoopR: Access Men's Basketball Play by Play Data — hoopR-package
title: hoopR: Access Men's Basketball Play by Play Data — hoopR-package
text: utility quickly obtain clean tidy men's basketball play play data. Provides functions access live play play box score data ESPNhttps://www.espn.com shot locations available. also full NBA Stats APIhttps://www.nba.com/stats/ wrapper. also scraping aggregating interface Ken Pomeroy's men's college basketball statistics websitehttps://kenpom.com. provides users active subscription capability scrape website tables analyze data .

title: hoopR: Access Men's Basketball Play by Play Data — hoopR-package
text: Maintainer: Saiem Gilani saiem.gilani@gmail.com (ORCID) contributors: Jason Lee Jason@aisportsfirm.com [contributor] Billy Fryer billyfryer@att.net [contributor] Ross Drucker ross..drucker@gmail.com [contributor] Vladislav Shufinskiy hollywood.90210@mail.ru [contributor]

what: Get Player Stats Leaders by Metric — kp_playerstats
title: Get Player Stats Leaders by Metric — kp_playerstats
text: Get Player Stats Leaders Metric

title: Get Player Stats Leaders by Metric — kp_playerstats
code: kp_playerstats(   metric = "eFG",   conf = NULL,   conf_only = FALSE,   year = most_recent_mbb_season() )

title: Get Player Stats Leaders by Metric — kp_playerstats
text: metric Used get leaders different metrics. Available values :  'ORtg', 'Min', 'eFG', 'Poss', Shots', '', 'DR', '', 'ARate', 'Blk',  'FTRate', 'Stl', 'TS', 'FC40', 'FD40', '2P', '3P', 'FT'.  Default 'eFG'. 'ORtg' returns list four dataframes, four tables:  players used 28 percent possessions, 24 percent possessions, 20 percent possessions, possession restriction. conf Used limit players specific conference. Allowed values : 'A10', 'ACC', 'AE', 'AMER',  'ASUN', 'B10', 'B12', '', 'BSKY', 'BSTH', 'BW', 'CAA', 'CUSA', 'HORZ', 'IND', IVY',  'MAAC', 'MAC', 'MEAC', 'MVC', 'MWC', 'NEC', 'OVC', 'P12', 'PAT', 'SB', 'SC', 'SEC', 'SLND',  'SUM', 'SWAC', 'WAC', 'WCC'.  try use conference exist given season, like 'IND' '2018',  get empty table, kenpom.com serve 404 pages invalid table queries like . filter applied default. conf_only Used define whether stats reflect conference games . available specific conference defined. available season 2013, FALSE default. year Year data pull (earliest year data available: 2004)

title: Get Player Stats Leaders by Metric — kp_playerstats
text: Returns tibble player stats

title: KenPom Login and Password credentials — kp_user_pw
code: login(user_email = Sys.getenv("KP_USER"), user_pw = Sys.getenv("KP_PW"))  kp_user_email()  kp_password()  has_kp_user_and_pw()

title: KenPom Login and Password credentials — kp_user_pw
text: user_email User subscription e-mail user_pw User subscription password

title: KenPom Login and Password credentials — kp_user_pw
text: Using KenPom subscription package: Run usethis::edit_r_environ() paste following new script pops (without quotations)   can save login information consistent usage adding
code: KP_USER = YOUR-EMAIL@DOMAIN.COM KP_PW = XXX-YOUR-PASSWORD-XXX KP_USER = YOUR-EMAIL@DOMAIN.COM KP_PW = XXX-YOUR-PASSWORD-XXX ``` \cr to your .REnviron file (easily accessed via [**`usethis::edit_r_environ()`**](https://usethis.r-lib.org/reference/edit.html)).\cr \cr  For less consistent usage:\cr At the beginning of every session or within an R environment, save your login e-mail and password as the environment variables `KP_USER` and `KP_PW` (with quotations) using a command like the following.\cr ```{r} Sys.setenv(KP_USER = "YOUR-EMAIL@DOMAIN.COM") Sys.setenv(KP_PW = "XXX-YOUR-PASSWORD-XXX")

text: dbdir Directory database shall located dbname File name existing desired SQLite database within dbdir tblname name play play data table within database force_rebuild Hybrid parameter (logical numeric) rebuild parts complete play play data table within database (please see details information) db_connection DBIConnection object, returned DBI::dbConnect() (please see details information)

title: Update or create a hoopR MBB play-by-play database — update_mbb_db
text: Returns logical value (TRUE/FALSE)

title: Update or create a hoopR MBB play-by-play database — update_mbb_db
text: function creates updates data table name tblname within SQLite database (drivers via db_connection) located dbdir named dbname. data table combines play play data every available game back 2006 season adds recent completed games soon available hoopR. argument force_rebuild hybrid type. can rebuild play play data table either whole hoopR era (force_rebuild = TRUE) just specified seasons (e.g. force_rebuild = c(2019, 2020)). Please note following behavior: force_rebuild = TRUE: data table name tblname removed completely rebuilt scratch. helpful new columns added -Season. force_rebuild = c(2019, 2020): data table name tblname preserved rows 2019 2020 seasons deleted re-added. intended used ongoing seasons ESPN's data provider can make changes underlying data week. parameter db_connection intended advanced users want use DBI drivers, MariaDB, Postgres odbc. Please note arguments dbdir dbname dropped case db_connection provided argument tblname still used write data table database.

title: hoopR 3.0.0
text: nba_playbyplayv3() function added — dedicated wrapper NBA Stats PlayByPlayV3 endpoint. nba_pbp() nba_pbps() now default version = "v3" (previously "v2"). Pass version = "v2" use previous behavior. .players_on_court_v3() internal helper added — enriches V3 play--play data -court player IDs parsing V3 substitution descriptions using nba_boxscoretraditionalv3() fallback initial lineup determination.

text: nba_boxscoresummaryv3() function added — returns named list 9 data frames: GameSummary, GameInfo, ArenaInfo, Officials, LineScore, InactivePlayers, LastFiveMeetings, OtherStats, AvailableVideo.

what: New NBA Stats API Endpoint Wrappers
title: hoopR 3.0.0
text: nba_commonteamyears() function added — returns team IDs active year ranges. nba_dunkscoreleaders() function added — returns dunk tracking data biomechanics, scores, style metrics. nba_gravityleaders() function added — returns gravity scores measuring much defensive attention player draws. nba_iststandings() function added — returns -Season Tournament (NBA Cup) standings. nba_scheduleleaguev2int() function added — returns international schedule data broadcaster information. nba_teamandplayersvsplayers() function added — returns team player lineup comparison stats (5 datasets). nba_videoeventsasset() function added — returns video event asset data given game event.

title: hoopR 3.0.0
text: espn_nba_team_current_roster() function added — returns current team roster ESPN. espn_mbb_team_current_roster() function added — returns current MBB team roster ESPN. nbagl_live_pbp() function added — returns live G-League play--play data. nbagl_live_boxscore() function added — returns live G-League box score data.

Fixed helper-skip.R test guard functions use proper string comparison (!= "1") instead numeric comparison (== 0). Fixed nba_dunkscoreleaders() HTTP 400 error caused empty string parameters. Fixed V3-style data.frame parsing leader/standings endpoints. Fixed nba_iststandings() nested games column flattening. Fixed %||% import R < 4.4.0 compatibility.
